conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll gather the necessary information from you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 3 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! It's helpful to know about Alex.

Now, to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. So you live in the UK and Alex lives with you.

Next question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is there anyone else who might also consider themselves responsible for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment. **Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one final question to ensure I have all the information needed:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any other living arrangement away from home for an extended period, or does he live with you full-time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he lives with me full-time. He's not in hospital or care or anything like that - just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- You have a son, Alex, who is 3 years old
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you full-time at home
- You are responsible for Alex
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- You are a UK citizen
- Alex is not in hospital or care

According to the UK government's Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually considered responsible for a child if you live with them.

**You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements because:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex and he lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- You are a UK citizen

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit. Please note that if either you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit regardless.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives full-time with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), the claimant is responsible for him, the claimant is a UK citizen with the right to reside, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child, they live in the UK, and only one person is claiming for the child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 3-year-old son Alex. Alex lives with them full-time and is not in hospital or care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
